add_seed.ip:
Required tools for the new seed (each called on THIS seed value):
  - defuse(ip, 1.2.3.4)
  - rdap_ip(1.2.3.4)
  - virustotal_ip(1.2.3.4)
  - shodan_host(1.2.3.4)  (passive — JARM, banners)
  - onyphe_ip(1.2.3.4)  (passive — banners, technologies)
  - reverse_dns(1.2.3.4)
  - virustotal_resolutions_ip(1.2.3.4)
  - virustotal_communicating_files("ip", 1.2.3.4)
  - threatfox_search(1.2.3.4)
  - otx_ip(1.2.3.4)
  - If a non-CDN JARM is found: shodan_search("ssl.jarm:<jarm>")
add_seed.domain:
Required tools for the new seed (each called on THIS seed value):
  - rdap_domain(evil.com) / dns_resolve(evil.com)
  - crtsh_subdomains(evil.com)
  - virustotal_domain(evil.com)
  - virustotal_resolutions_domain(evil.com)
  - virustotal_communicating_files("domain", evil.com)
  - threatfox_search(evil.com)
  - otx_domain(evil.com)
  - urlhaus_host(evil.com)
  - onyphe_domain(evil.com)  (passive fingerprinting)
add_seed.hash:
Required tools for the new seed (each called on THIS seed value):
  - malwarebazaar_hash(d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e)
  - virustotal_file(d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e)
  - otx_file(d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e)
  - threatfox_search(d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e)
For the hash node set metadata.file_name (required for UI labels).
add_seed.executable_name:
This is a filename-only add-seed (no binary, no hash). Required:
  - malwarebazaar_filename(dropper.exe)  — top samples → graph each
    as a hash node with an `observed_as` edge to the executable_name.
    Top 3 samples: also virustotal_file + otx_file + malwarebazaar_hash
    to pull family / C2 / file_name set.
  - threatfox_search(dropper.exe)
If any returned sample's sha256 / family / C2 ALREADY exists on the
graph (from a prior seed), that's a concrete cross-seed link — record
it in cross_seed_findings.
add_seed.url:
This is a URL add-seed. Graph the URL as a url node with tags=['seed'],
derive the host, graph it as domain/ip, then run the full host workflow
(rdap, dns, VT, threatfox, otx, urlhaus, urlscan, JARM).
add_seed.jarm:
This is a JARM fingerprint add-seed. Required tools:
  - shodan_search("ssl.jarm:aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa")  — enumerate cluster
  - urlscan_search("hash:aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa")  — cross-source confirmation
  - threatfox_search(aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa)
  - For top 3 diverse IPs: defuse + rdap_ip + virustotal_ip + threatfox_search
For every host with this JARM: add_node(ip) + add_edge(ip→jarm, has_jarm).
If a cluster IP ALREADY exists on the graph (same id as a prior seed's infra),
that's a concrete cross-seed link — record it in cross_seed_findings.
add_seed.asn:
This is an ASN add-seed. Required tools:
  - shodan_search("asn:AS13335 port:443")
  - For top 5 interesting IPs: defuse + virustotal_ip + threatfox_search + otx_ip
  - rdap_ip on ONE representative IP (netname/country/abuse_email)
  - threatfox_search("AS13335")
If multiple hosts in the AS share a JARM, graph that JARM and link all hits.
If any cluster IP is ALREADY on the graph, record it in cross_seed_findings.
add_seed.email:
This is an email add-seed. Required tools:
  - emailrep_check(a@b.com)
  - whoxy_reverse(email="a@b.com")  — every reverse-WHOIS domain hit
    becomes a domain node + registered edge from the email.
  - pulsedive_indicator(a@b.com)
  - opencti_lookup_indicator(a@b.com)
  - threatfox_search(a@b.com)
If any returned domain ALREADY exists on the graph, that's a concrete
cross-seed link — record it in cross_seed_findings.
add_seed.wallet_address:
This is a wallet_address add-seed. Required tools:
  - threatfox_search(0xabc)
  - pulsedive_indicator(0xabc)
  - opencti_lookup_indicator(0xabc)
  - urlscan_search("0xabc")  — phishing page DOM may include it
Set metadata.chain on the wallet node (btc / eth / xmr / …).
add_seed.username:
This is a username add-seed. Required tools:
  - threatfox_search(h4ck3r)
  - pulsedive_indicator(h4ck3r)
  - opencti_lookup_indicator(h4ck3r)
  - urlscan_search("h4ck3r")
Every domain / IP / hash co-mentioned with the handle becomes a node
with a uses_handle edge to the username.

pivot.ip:
Call these tools (skip any whose results are already in the graph):
  - rdap_ip(1.2.3.4)
  - virustotal_ip(1.2.3.4)
  - shodan_host(1.2.3.4)  (passive — extract JARM, banners, technologies)
  - onyphe_ip(1.2.3.4)  (passive — banners, cert, technologies)
  - reverse_dns(1.2.3.4)
  - virustotal_resolutions_ip(1.2.3.4)
  - virustotal_communicating_files("ip", 1.2.3.4)
  - threatfox_search(1.2.3.4)
  - otx_ip(1.2.3.4)
If a JARM is extracted and it is not a well-known CDN JARM, also call
  - shodan_search("ssl.jarm:<jarm>") and add new IPs with same_jarm edges.
pivot.domain:
Call these tools (skip any whose results are already in the graph):
  - rdap_domain(evil.com) / dns_resolve(evil.com)
  - crtsh_subdomains(evil.com)
  - virustotal_domain(evil.com)
  - virustotal_resolutions_domain(evil.com)
  - virustotal_communicating_files("domain", evil.com)
  - threatfox_search(evil.com)
  - otx_domain(evil.com)
  - onyphe_domain(evil.com)  (passive fingerprinting)
pivot.hash:
Call these tools (skip any whose results are already in the graph):
  - malwarebazaar_hash(d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e)
  - virustotal_file(d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e)
  - otx_file(d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e)
  - threatfox_search(d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e)
For every hash node created or updated, set metadata.file_name.
pivot.executable_name:
This is a filename-only pivot (no binary, no hash). Required:
  - malwarebazaar_filename(dropper.exe)  — graph each returned
    sample's sha256 as a hash node + observed_as edge to the
    executable_name. Top 3 samples: also virustotal_file +
    malwarebazaar_hash + otx_file to pull family + C2.
  - threatfox_search(dropper.exe)
pivot.url:
This is a URL pivot. Graph the URL as a url node (tag as seed if new),
extract the host and graph it as domain/ip node. Then run enrichment on
the host as you would for a domain/ip pivot:
  - urlscan_search("page.url:http://evil.com/x")
  - urlhaus_host(<host>)
  - rdap + DNS + VT (domain or ip flavor, depending on host)
  - threatfox_search on both the URL and the host
pivot.jarm:
This is a JARM pivot. Call these tools (skip any already in graph):
  - shodan_search("ssl.jarm:aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa")  — find cluster hosts
  - urlscan_search("hash:aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa")
  - threatfox_search(aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa)
For each new IP with this JARM: add_node(ip) + add_edge(ip→jarm, has_jarm).
For top 3 IPs: defuse + virustotal_ip + threatfox_search.
pivot.asn:
This is an ASN pivot. Call these tools (skip any already in graph):
  - shodan_search("asn:AS13335 port:443")
  - rdap_ip on one representative IP for netname/country/abuse_email
  - threatfox_search("AS13335")
For top 5 interesting IPs in the AS: defuse + virustotal_ip + threatfox_search.
Tag the asn 'abused_asn' when ≥2 of those hosts return detection hits.
pivot.email:
This is an email pivot. Call these tools (skip any already in graph):
  - emailrep_check(a@b.com)
  - whoxy_reverse(email="a@b.com")
  - pulsedive_indicator(a@b.com)
  - opencti_lookup_indicator(a@b.com)
  - threatfox_search(a@b.com)
For each new domain returned by whoxy: add_node + registered edge.
pivot.wallet_address:
This is a wallet_address pivot. Call these tools (skip already-graphed):
  - threatfox_search(0xabc)
  - pulsedive_indicator(0xabc)
  - opencti_lookup_indicator(0xabc)
  - urlscan_search("0xabc")
pivot.username:
This is a username pivot. Call these tools (skip already-graphed):
  - threatfox_search(h4ck3r)
  - pulsedive_indicator(h4ck3r)
  - opencti_lookup_indicator(h4ck3r)
  - urlscan_search("h4ck3r")
